auth() { curl -sS -H "Authorization: token $GITHUB_TOKEN" -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" "$@"; }
MAIN=0ce421f71946af345bc1e971ff2b0713d4aeede5
BRANCH=agent/dm-subband-tilt-figure

# append our row onto MAIN's current manifest
cp /tmp/main_manifest.csv /tmp/rebuilt_manifest.csv
python3 - <<'PY'
import csv
row=[
  "figures/dm_subband_tilt.png","figure","no",
  "pipeline/dispersion/dm_campaign/render_dm_zoom_comparison.py",
  "cd pipeline && PYTHONPATH=. python -m dispersion.dm_campaign.render_dm_zoom_comparison --fits ../analysis/dm-joint-phase-v2/results/fits.json --out ../figures/dm_subband_tilt.png --bursts phineas isha hamilton --window-ms 12 --mode tilt --n-sub 4",
  "yes","blocked_external_data",
  ("Sub-band arrival-time (tilt) diagnostic for the CHIME-primary DM adoption: per-sub-band on-pulse arrival "
   "time vs frequency for phineas, isha (the two discrepant sightlines) and hamilton (control), each band "
   "dedispersed at both candidate DMs on a shared time ruler. CHIME cold-plasma expectations for the two DMs "
   "fan out by ~0.8-1.6 ms; DSA expectations collapse to a sub-sample stripe. Inputs: the 6 relevant "
   "~/Data/Faber2026/dsa110/DSA_bursts/*_cntr_bpc.npy waterfalls and analysis/dm-joint-phase-v2/results/fits.json. "
   "ADDED 2026-07-15 (Faber2026 #83). PRODUCER NOT YET IN PIN: render_dm_zoom_comparison.py lands in the pipeline "
   "submodule via FLITS #185 (branch agent/dm-zoom-diagnostic-script); clone-reproducible only after #185 merges "
   "and the submodule pin is bumped to a commit that includes it. External raw data also prevent fresh-clone execution.")
]
with open('/tmp/rebuilt_manifest.csv','a',newline='') as f:
    csv.writer(f).writerow(row)
rows=list(csv.reader(open('/tmp/rebuilt_manifest.csv')))
assert all(len(r)==8 for r in rows) and len(rows)-1==30, (len(rows), [len(r) for r in rows[-2:]])
print("rebuilt manifest rows:", len(rows)-1, "OK")
PY

# blobs off main tree
BASETREE=$(auth "$API/repos/$REPO/git/commits/$MAIN" | python3 -c "import sys,json;print(json.load(sys.stdin)['tree']['sha'])")
base64 < figures/dm_subband_tilt.png > /tmp/tf_png.txt   # local = fixed deb9702 bytes
base64 < /tmp/rebuilt_manifest.csv    > /tmp/tf_man.txt
b_png=$(python3 -c "import json;print(json.dumps({'content':open('/tmp/tf_png.txt').read().replace(chr(10),''),'encoding':'base64'}))" | auth -X POST "$API/repos/$REPO/git/blobs" -d @- | python3 -c "import sys,json;print(json.load(sys.stdin)['sha'])")
b_man=$(python3 -c "import json;print(json.dumps({'content':open('/tmp/tf_man.txt').read().replace(chr(10),''),'encoding':'base64'}))" | auth -X POST "$API/repos/$REPO/git/blobs" -d @- | python3 -c "import sys,json;print(json.load(sys.stdin)['sha'])")
TREE=$(python3 -c "
import json;print(json.dumps({'base_tree':'$BASETREE','tree':[
 {'path':'figures/dm_subband_tilt.png','mode':'100644','type':'blob','sha':'$b_png'},
 {'path':'repro_manifest.csv','mode':'100644','type':'blob','sha':'$b_man'}]}))" | auth -X POST "$API/repos/$REPO/git/trees" -d @- | python3 -c "import sys,json;print(json.load(sys.stdin)['sha'])")
MSG="fig(dm tilt): corrected sub-band tilt figure + repro manifest row (rebuilt on main)

main independently gained a buggy (mirrored-slope) copy of
figures/dm_subband_tilt.png via #86; this replaces it with the corrected
render (cold-plasma overlay sign fix, FLITS #185 40cb5c7) and adds the
figures/dm_subband_tilt.png row to repro_manifest.csv. Rebuilt on current
main (0ce421f) to merge cleanly."
COMMIT=$(python3 -c "import json,sys;print(json.dumps({'message':sys.argv[1],'tree':'$TREE','parents':['$MAIN']}))" "$MSG" | auth -X POST "$API/repos/$REPO/git/commits" -d @- | python3 -c "import sys,json;print(json.load(sys.stdin)['sha'])")
auth -X PATCH "$API/repos/$REPO/git/refs/heads/$BRANCH" -d "{\"sha\":\"$COMMIT\",\"force\":true}" | python3 -c "import sys,json;d=json.load(sys.stdin);print('branch now at:', d.get('object',{}).get('sha','ERR')[:12] if 'object' in d else d.get('message'))"
echo "new commit: $COMMIT"

echo "=== what does test_journal_append.sh check? (most likely branch-only failure) ==="
sed -n '1,40p' tests/test_journal_append.sh 2>/dev/null | grep -vE "^\s*#" | grep -vE "^\s*$" | head -25
stdout:
=== what does test_journal_append.sh check? (most likely branch-only failure) ===
set -euo pipefail
ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
TMP="$(mktemp -d)"
trap 'rm -rf "$TMP"' EXIT
mkdir -p "$TMP/scripts" "$TMP/docs/rse"
cp "$ROOT/scripts/journal-append.sh" "$TMP/scripts/journal-append.sh"
: > "$TMP/docs/rse/journal.jsonl"
if bash "$TMP/scripts/journal-append.sh" "test-agent" "repo" "finish" "bad state" >/tmp/journal-invalid.out 2>/tmp/journal-invalid.err; then
  echo "expected invalid state to fail" >&2
  exit 1
fi
if [[ -s "$TMP/docs/rse/journal.jsonl" ]]; then
  echo "invalid state should not append to journal" >&2
  exit 1
fi
bash "$TMP/scripts/journal-append.sh" "test-agent" "repo" "done" "valid state" >/tmp/journal-valid.out
python3 - "$TMP/docs/rse/journal.jsonl" <<'PY'
import json
import sys
entries = [json.loads(line) for line in open(sys.argv[1])]
assert len(entries) == 1, entries
entry = entries[0]
assert entry["agent"] == "test-agent", entry
assert entry["lane"] == "repo", entry
assert entry["state"] == "done", entry
